variableLongName: Particle backscattering at 880 nanometers.
Downwelling vector irradiance as energy of electromagnetic radiation (PAR wavelengths) in the water body by cosine-collector radiometer.
unitLongName: MicroEinsteins per square metre per second

variableLongName: Particle backscattering at a specified optical wavelength.
The pressure that the oxygen component of an atmosphere sample would have if it alone occupied the same volume at the same temperature as the sample.
Total angle specific volume from backscattering sensor at 880 nanometers.
The vertical component of light at 412nm wavelength travelling downwards.
unitLongName: Watts per square metre per nanometre

variableLongName: Total angle specific volume from backscattering sensor at 650 nanometers.
The depth-averaged current in the x-axis using dead reckoning between two surfacings.
The vertical component of light at 443nm wavelength travelling downwards.
unitLongName: Watts per square metre per nanometre

variableLongName: Total angle specific volume from backscattering sensor at unspecified wavelength.
Pressure (spatial co-ordinate) exerted by the water body by profiling pressure sensor and corrected to read zero at sea level
Longitude east by unspecified GPS system.
Particle backscattering at 700 nanometers.

The difference between the volume of the internal variable buoyancy device (VBD) on a glider and the volume of the VBD that would maintain neutral buoyancy. If values are positive then the glider rises in the water.

The volume of oil in the external reservoir of the platform. A positive change in OIL_VOL represents moving oil from the internal reservoir (within the pressure casing) to the external reservoir, increasing the total volume of the platform.
Pressure (spatial co-ordinate) exerted by the water body by non temperature compensated platform pressure sensor and corrected to read zero at sea level.
Total angle specific volume from backscattering sensor at 470 nanometers.
The depth-averaged current in the y-axis using dead reckoning between two surfacings.
The vertical component of light at 655nm wavelength travelling downwards.
unitLongName: Watts per square metre per nanometre

variableLongName: Latitude north.
Particle backscattering at 470 nanometers.
The vertical component of light at 555nm wavelength travelling downwards.
unitLongName: Watts per square metre per nanometre

variableLongName: Total angle specific volume from backscattering sensor at 532 nanometers.

Derived from the glider's engineering state, glider phase reflects the state of glider behaviour. Accepted values range from 0 - 7, with the definitions available at: https://github.com/OceanGlidersCommunity/OG-format-user-manual/blob/main/vocabularyCollection/phase_translation_tables.md.
Time elapsed since since start of glider yo.
The vertical component of light at the specified wavelength travelling downwards.
unitLongName: Watts per square metre per nanometre

variableLongName: The raw output from a dissolved oxygen sensor such as an SBE43 with output as a voltage related to DO concentration.
Latitude north by unspecified GPS system.
The distance of a glider's internal pitch mass from the position that would maintain neutral pitch (Positive nose up).
Time elapsed since since start of day.
The vertical component of light at 532nm wavelength travelling downwards.
unitLongName: Watts per square metre per nanometre

variableLongName: The vertical component of light at 490nm wavelength travelling downwards.
unitLongName: Watts per square metre per nanometre

variableLongName: Total angle specific volume from backscattering sensor at 700 nanometers.
The partial pressure of oxygen in air in equilibrium with a water sample.
Water body dissolved oxygen concentration expressed at zero rather than in-situ salinity. This is NOT an environment state variable.
Electrical conductivity of the water body by CTD
Phase delay reported by oxygen sensor.
Voltage of instrument output by in-situ thermistor
Turbidity signal from side scattering sensor.
Particle backscattering at 650 nanometers.
Time elapsed since 1970-01-01T00:00:00Z.
The quantity of coloured organic compounds in solution in unit volume of any body of water.
Phycocyanin concentration per unit volume of fresh or salt water.
Particle backscattering at 412 nanometers.
Estimate of suspended sediment concentration based on the proportion of a light transmission in the water column that is reflected back to a co-located receiver.
The raw output from a dissolved oxygen sensor such as an SBE43 with output as a frequency related to DO concentration.
Particle backscattering at 715 nanometers.
Longitude east.
Phycoerythrin concentration per unit volume of fresh or salt water.
Particle backscattering at 532 nanometers.

The quantity of the optically measurable component of coloured (chromophoric) dissolved organic matter (CDOM) that fluoresces when excited with near ultra-violet (UV) light, measured in a unit volume of salt water by calibration against a quinine sulphate proxy. Often referred to as fluorescent dissolved organic matter (FDOM) when expressed as quinine sulphate equivalent.
The vertical component of light at 380nm wavelength travelling downwards.
unitLongName: Watts per square metre per nanometre

variableLongName: Potential temperature of the water body by computation using UNESCO 1983 algorithm.
Internal temperature (degC) of the conductivity cell.
Temperature of oxygen determination by optode.
Saturation of oxygen {O2 CAS 7782-44-7} in the water body [dissolved plus reactive particulate phase] by in-situ oxygen optode and computation from concentration.
The rate at which sound travels through the water column.
The depth-averaged current in the y-axis using dead reckoning between two surfacings. Corrected for surface drift while the glider receives its first GPS fix at the surface.
The depth-averaged current in the x-axis using dead reckoning between two surfacings. Corrected for surface drift while the glider receives its first GPS fix at the surface.
D phase is calculated as a third degree polynomial of (the phase angle measured by blue excitation light minus the phase angle measured by red excitation light).
Uncalibrated blue excitation light phase shift reported by oxygen sensor.
The tilting rotation of a platform relative to its longitudinal/X axis as measured by an inclinometer.
Uncalibrated red excitation light phase shift reported by oxygen sensor.
The rotation of a platform relative to its upward/Z axis relative to magnetic north. Often referred to as yaw or bearing.
Uncalibrated (temperature compensated) phase shift reported by oxygen sensor.
The up/down rotation of a platform relative to it Y axis as measured by an inclinometer.
The rotation of a platform relative to its upward/Z axis relative to True North. Often referred to as yaw or bearing.
Sigma-theta of the water body and computation from salinity and potential temperature using UNESCO algorithm.
Potential density of the water body by computation from salinity and theta using UNESCO algorithm with 0 decibar reference pressure
The mass of a unit volume of any body of fresh or salt water.
Computed by UNESCO SVAN function using in-situ temperature.
The raw output expressed in counts from an upward-facing cosine collector radiometer for optical wavelength 380nm.
The square root of the average of the squares of deviations about the mean of counts in the water body by a turbulence profiler shear sensor.
The raw output expressed in counts from an upward-facing cosine collector radiometer for optical wavelength 443nm.
The square root of the average of the squares of deviations about the mean of counts in the water body by a second turbulence profiler thermistor.

Intensity of the echo return from an altimeter, ranging from 0 to 1. A value of 1 represents full saturation of the receiver. An energy level of 0.707 (square root of 2) is the theoretical perfect level as it represents the energy of a pure sine wave with an amplitude utilising the maximum dynamic range of the receiver.
The raw analogue-to-digital converter output from a temperature sensor mounted on a CPU that is a component part of a radiometer.
Glider command phase.
The square root of the average of the squares of deviations about the mean of counts in the water body by a turbulence profiler thermistor.
Practical salinity of the water body by CTD and computation using UNESCO 1983 algorithm
The average number of counts in the water body across multiple measurements by a second turbulence profiler thermistor.
Count which is expressive of uncalibrated temperature value reported by oxygen sensor.
The average number of counts in the water body across multiple measurements by a turbulence profiler thermistor.
The variable buoyancy device (VBD) A/D count position of a glider.
The raw output expressed in counts from an upward-facing cosine collector radiometer for one unspecified wavelength from the measured spectrum.
The raw output expressed in counts from an upward-facing cosine collector radiometer for optical wavelength 490nm.
Quality of the echo return from an altimeter, ranging from 0 to 1. A value of 1 represents a perfect return echo with negligible noise and distortion.
The raw output expressed in counts from an upward-facing cosine collector radiometer for optical wavelength 555nm.
The roll A/D count position of a glider internal mass.
The angle of a glider's internal roll mass from the position that would maintain neutral roll (Positive starboard wing down).
The square root of the average of the squares of deviations about the mean of counts in the water body by a second turbulence profiler shear sensor.
The raw output expressed in counts from an upward-facing cosine collector radiometer for optical wavelength 412nm.
Engineering data cycle number.
Phycoerythrin signal from a fluorescence sensor.

The amount of violet to low ultra-violet radiation generated in the water column in response to high ultra-violet radiation transmission expressed relative to an unspecified baseline using fluorometer designed to detect CDOM, crude oil or UV-fluorescent dyes.
Number of times oxygen sensor flashing to measure oxygen.
The raw output expressed in counts from an upward-facing cosine collector radiometer measuring the integrated light over PAR wavelengths.
Raw fluorescence signal (count) from chlorophyll-a fluorometer.
The raw analogue-to-digital converter output from a temperature sensor mounted on a CPU that is a component part of a backscattering meter and/or fluorometer.
Raw backscattering sensor signal (count) at 700 nm wavelength. Volume scattering scale factor has not been applied.
Number of values included in the determination of a statistic such as an average.
The raw output from a dissolved oxygen sensor such as an SBE43 with output converted from voltage or frequency into counts by an ADC.
The pitch A/D count position of a glider internal mass.
Concentration of dissolved oxygen per unit mass of the water column. Oxygen may be expressed in terms of mass, volume or quantity of substance.
Concentration of dissolved oxygen per unit volume of the water column. Oxygen may be expressed in terms of mass, volume or quantity of substance.
Distance in meters from the transducer face to the target.
Depth below surface of the water body by unknown instrument and correction to zero at sea level using unspecified algorithm.
The vertical distance of a platform above the bed surface of a water body.
In-situ fluorometer with either manufacturer, laboratory or sample calibration applied.
Temperature of the water body by CTD or STD
